author: parthsingh2006
content:
hey everyone!

as we are almost at the end of week 2, i am writing this to give a small update regarding my learnings as our official dev updates would take some time (would complete it by this weekend)

me and my friend <@1335660981019480075>  spent another week &went deeper into reading about the pureth eips and all the resources we could find like dev talks, previous cohort impls, notes, etc

initially we were looking into the reth partial statefulness side but it was very saturated, so after researching the scope with all the info we can and discussing it with the mentor we moved more seriously towards pureth

i have been leaning towards SSZ execution blocks
and arsh towards SSZ query language (both in reth)
since both of these projects are closely related and sort of depend on each other so we have been trying to understand the boundaries and how collaboration can happen here.

we also talked to other fellows who were interested in similar topics and made clear of our intentions and direction of work, basically just to sort things out early before any clash happens or directions of work overlap on any of the ends..

and since the topics are closely related we also talked to the mentor regarding the collaboration and how we are gonna work things out and he welcomed the suggestion.

will post the detailed updates on the channel soon.
also we would love to hear from you guys if any of you are also looking into these topics or with anything around pureth. 
thanks!!

author: krisoshea.eth
content: Hey <@921369499733725205> yes I’m looking into the builder as my main project now and may contribute to deathstar too if I can contribute something from what I / we learn when we build our that project. Ye sounds good, I’m still deep in research and trying to fully grok the project but as it stands I would like to work on the builder and have done some basic research so far so we can defo connect to discuss if you want 🙂 I still have a lot of research to do though as I’m still diving deep into the topic and the project scope
